prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first. A committed task
    force pulled off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line; a REINFORCE or DISBAND line IS that
TF's one line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed
UNASSIGNED units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept).
To rebuild membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED)
and FORM anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED
units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. A BUILD line is optional per
city — no BUILD line means the city keeps its current build (changing
discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-1  formed t38 · STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"
    since: holding along column 5 since t44; no contact; no losses
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-3  formed t38 · PATROL (7,2) — "screen the crossing lane"
    since: on station; t52: an enemy transport slipped in along the south
      coast and unloaded two armies at (3,4) and (5,4), next to city (4,3)

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-o = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
     X Y Z = enemy units in sight NOW (see KNOWN ENEMY)
 r0  . i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . . b . O d ~ ~ ~ ~ ? E ? ?
 r2  . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?
 r3  . . . . O f ~ o ~ ~ . . . ?
 r4  . . . X h Y ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ Z ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (5,0)                        TF-1
  d  #4   army       (5,1)                        TF-1
  e  #5   army       (5,2)                        TF-1
  f  #6   army       (5,3)                        TF-1
  g  #7   army       (4,2)                        TF-1
  h  #8   army       (4,4)                        TF-1
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (4,3) in city, NEW t52       UNASSIGNED
  n  #9   transport  (7,2) empty                  TF-3
  o  #10  destroyer  (7,3)                        TF-3
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 3 turns left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 2 turns left
  (4,3) building ARMY, 5 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 29 turns left (captured t51 — #14 spent)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,1); city (11,2); destroyer at (8,3) seen at t49
  X  enemy army at (3,4) — LANDED t52 from a transport
  Y  enemy army at (5,4) — LANDED t52 from a transport
  Z  enemy transport offshore at (4,5), now empty

It is TURN 52. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.content:
TF-1: REINFORCE UNITS i j | Prepare reserves for eastern strike when transport available
TF-2: REINFORCE UNITS k | Consolidate capital defense
TF-3: CONTINUE | Maintain crossing lane screen and escort capability
message.reasoning_content:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** Strategic commander (Task Force level). Do not micromanage units. Give objectives to Task Forces.
    *   **Format:** Strictly follow the "ORDERS CONTRACT". Output ONLY lines in specific forms (`TF <id>: ...`, `FORM TF ...`, `BUILD ...`). No commentary, headers, or prose.
    *   **Context:** Turn 52, Farshore Game State provided.
    *   **Map & Units:**
        *   Map: Grid with Land/Water/Fog/Cities.
            *   (0,1) is city `O`? Wait, looking at map legend: `r0`, `r1`, etc.
            *   Row 4 has enemies `X` (3,4), `Y` (5,4). Neutral cities are none known. Enemy cities at (11,1), (11,2).
            *   User cities (O): (1,2) [building Trans], (4,3) [building Army], (2,0) [Building Army] - Wait, map says `O` at (2,0)? Map shows `r2 . O . . g ...`. Unit list says `a #1 army (2,0) in city TF-2`. Unit `k #13 army (2,0) in city UNASSIGNED`.
            *   Wait, the map legend says `O my city`. But there are units inside.
            *   Let's check City Status:
                *   (2,0): Building ARMY, 3 turns left. (TF-2 garrisons this).
                *   (1,2): Building TRANSPORT, 2 turns left.
                *   (4,3): Building ARMY, 5 turns left. (Known Enemy Army X and Y are nearby at (3,4) and (5,4)).
                *   (4,1): Captured t51? No, Map says "N" neutral city in legend but text says "NEUTRAL CITIES none known". Wait, map line `r2 . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?`. 'n' looks like a neutral city symbol? But the rules say "A neutral city can be captured... only an army captures a city". The unit list shows `l #15 army (4,3) in city UNASSIGNED` and `m #17 army (4,3) in city NEW t52`. Wait, (4,3) is User City? Map says `O` at (4,3)? Let's check map: `r3 . O . . O f ...`. Ah, `r2` has an `E` at col 4? No, `r2 . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?`. Wait, the provided text is a bit garbled visually but I need to parse the data provided.
            *   Map Grid Analysis:
                *   `O` = My City.
                *   `E` = Enemy City.
                *   `N`/`n`? Legend says `N neutral city`. But rule says `NEUTRAL CITIES none known`. I should treat them as empty or capture-able. The prompt says "none known" for Neutral Cities, but map shows 'n'. I will assume my cities are (2,0), (1,2), (4,3) based on the unit list context where `a` is in city at (2,0). Wait, the map shows `O` at `(2,0)`, `(1,2)` is not marked O, wait. `r1 . . b . O d ...`. So `(1,2)` is a city? Legend: `O my city`. So `(1,2)` is `(1,2)`.
                *   Wait, the prompt map lines are:
                    *   r0: `. i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?` -> (0,2) is O? No, row 0 usually top. Let's assume standard matrix notation where first char is col 0? Or line 0? Usually `(x,y)` in text maps implies x=col, y=row.
                    *   Given `TF-1 formed t38 · STAGE (5,2)`. Map r2 has `(5,2)` as `~` (water)? Legend: `~ water`.
                    *   Let's check the unit positions vs map symbols carefully.
                    *   Unit `c` #3 army at (5,0). Map r0 col 5 is `~`. Wait, if y=0 is top row? Then `(5,0)` is water? But units can't be on water unless in city. Is it a typo or did I misinterpret the map?
                    *   Let's look at `(2,0)`: Map r0 col 2 is `O`. So City (2,0). Correct.
                    *   `(5,2)`: Map r2 col 5 is `~` (water). But TF-1 stages there? Wait, TF-1 units `c,d,e,f,g,h` are listed as armies at (5,0), (5,1), (5,2), (5,3), (4,2), (4,4).
                    *   If (5,2) is water, how can an Army stage there?
                    *   Actually, let's look at the "Current Taskings": `TF-1 ... STAGE (5,2)`. This suggests (5,2) is a target. Maybe it's not water. Legend says `~ water`. Map r2 col 5 is `n`? No `r2 . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?`. Col 0: `.` (Land). Col 1: `O` (City). Col 2: `.` (Land). Col 3: `.` (Land). Col 4: `g` (Army #7 at 5,2? Wait).
                    *   Let's align Units and Map coordinates.

                            *   Why the tasking says STAGE (5,2)? Maybe it means "Keep staging here until condition met" or "Final destination is (5,2)". Given it says "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities".
                        *   Let's look at the threat: Enemy Transport unloaded two armies at (3,4) [X] and (5,4) [Y]. City nearby at (4,3). My City at (4,3) is `O`. Wait, map r4 col 3? No, let's check.

                            *   City at (4,3)? Legend says `(4,3) building ARMY`. Is it on map?

                            *   Ah, my previous parsing of rows was off by one row potentially.

                                *   y=1: r1. x=4 is 'O'. (City 4,1?). Legend says `(1,2) building TRANSPORT`. Is there an O at r1 c1? `r1 . . b . O d`. `.`(0), `.`(1), `b`(2), `.`(3), `O`(4). So City (4,1)? Wait.

                                    *   r0 c2: 'O'. Matches (2,0).
                                    *   r1 c4: 'O'. Matches... ? No legend says (1,2). Maybe y=0 is bottom? Or x,y swap?

                                        *   `r0` is y=0. `r3` is y=3.
                                        *   r0 c2 = 'O'. City (2,0).
                                        *   r1 c4 = 'O'. City (4,1)? Legend says (1,2). If I swap x/y? Then (2,1) would be City.
                                        *   Wait, `TF-3 ... PATROL (7,2)`. Map r2 c7. r2: `. O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?`. Length is 16 chars? No.

                                    *   My Units `TF-1`: `c,d,e,f,g,h` + others. Wait, units list says `c` #3 is at (5,0). Is (5,0) valid? Legend: `~ water`. Map r0 c5 is `~`. Unit `c` is at (5,0). This is a contradiction in the prompt's map vs unit data OR my interpretation.

                                        *   If (y, x):
                                            *   Unit `a` at (2,0). Row 2 Col 0. Map r2 c0 is `.`? No.
                                            *   Unit `a` is in City. Where is a city in r2? `r2 . O ...`. O is at col 1 or 2? `. O`. Col 1?

                                            *   City at (1,2): r2 c1 'O'. Map `r2 . O`. Yes! Col 1 is 'O'.
                                            *   City at (4,3): r3 c4 'O'. Map `r3 . . . . O`. Yes! Col 4 is 'O'.
                                            *   City at (4,1): r1 c4 'O'. Map `r1 . . b . O d`. Wait, Col 4 is 'O'. Yes!

                                                *   r1 = y=1.
                                                *   r2 = y=2.
                                                *   r3 = y=3.
                                                *   r4 = y=4.
                                                *   r5 = y=5.

                                                *   Indices: 0(.), 1(i), 2(O), 3(j), 4(.), 5(c).
                                                *   Wait, I counted 6 chars before `~`. `.` (0), `i` (1), `O` (2), `j` (3), `.` (4), `c` (5).
                                                *   Okay, so (5,0) is Land (`c`). The `~` symbols are further right.
                                                *   Wait, earlier I thought (5,0) was water because of the visual alignment in my head. Let's re-verify column count.
                                                *   r0: `. i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?`
                                                *   Chars: 1(2), 2(i), 3(O), 4(j), 5(.), 6(c)?
                                                *   Let's assume space-separated or just character indices. The prompt text doesn't use spaces between chars in the map legend block `r0  . i O j ...`. It looks like space separated.
                                                *   So r0 col 0=`.`, col 1=`i`, col 2=`O`, col 3=`j`, col 4=`.`, col 5=`c`.
                                                *   Okay, so (5,0) is valid Land. `~` starts at col 6.
                                            *   Okay, Map is consistent now. (x,y) = (col, row).
                                    *   Threats:
                                        *   Enemy Transport Z at (4,5). (r5 c4 = 'Z'?). Map r5: `~ ~ ~ ~ Z ~ ...`. Yes, Col 4. So (4,5) is Water/Offshore.
                                        *   Enemy Armies X at (3,4) (Land). Y at (5,4) (Land).
                                            *   City at (4,3). X and Y are adjacent to it.
                                            *   My City (4,3) is building Army (5 turns left). Currently has no garrison?
                                                *   Units at (4,3): `l #15 army (4,3) in city UNASSIGNED`, `m #17 army (4,3) in city NEW t52`. Wait, Unit list says: `l` and `m` are at (4,3).
                                                *   But "MY CITIES" line for (4,3) says "building ARMY". So it's not garrisoned yet? Or units in the cell block them?
                                                *   Wait, "UNASSIGNED" means they aren't part of a TF. Why are two armies in (4,3)? Maybe one is building and one is staging? Or captured?
                                                *   Actually, if (4,3) has units `l` and `m` listed as UNASSIGNED, but the city builds an Army...
                                                *   This seems to be a state where production happens while units exist.
                                    *   Current Tasking Issues:
                                        *   TF-1: STAGE (5,2). "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities".
                                            *   Units `c` through `h` are in TF-1. Where are they?
                                            *   List: `c(5,0), d(5,1), e(5,2), f(5,3), g(4,2), h(4,4)`.
                                            *   (5,2) is on Land? Map r2 c5. `r2 . O . . g e ~ n ...`. Col 4=g(#7), Col 5=e(#5). So (5,2) has Army `e`.
                                            *   Tasking says STAGE (5,2). Units are there. What is the "second transport"?
                                            *   I have `(1,2)` building TRANSPORT in 2 turns. I have `(4,1)` building TRANSPORT (Captured t51) in 29 turns.
                                            *   TF-3 is at `(7,2)` PATROL. `n` is transport #10? No `n` is army #11? No Unit list:
                                                *   `a #1 army`... `l #15 army`.
                                                *   `n #9 transport`. Wait, `i` is `#11`. `j` is `#12`.
                                                *   Transport at (7,2)? Map r2 c7 is `n`? No, `r2 . O . . g e ~ n ...`. `.`(0), `O`(1), `.`(2), `.`(3), `g`(4), `e`(5), `~`(6), `n`(7).
                                                *   So (7,2) has Transport `n` (#9). This matches TF-3.
                                            *   TF-3 says "screen the crossing lane". Enemy Transport Z is at (4,5). My Transport is at (7,2).
                                            *   Wait, `(4,1)` is neutral/captured? "MY CITIES: (4,1) building TRANSPORT... (captured t51 — #14 spent)".
